specific_drivers: Diesel (haulage, fleet); electricity (comminution, pumping, ventilation, electrolysis, arc furnaces); gas (process heat)
rationale: Often 25-40% of operating cost; grinding/smelting/electrolysis can be the single largest line item.

specific_drivers: Wages & on-costs; skill availability; FIFO/camp; productivity; automation
rationale: Major cost; autonomous haulage & remote ops are key levers, especially deep underground.

specific_drivers: Strip ratio (waste:ore); deferred stripping; UG development metres & backfill
rationale: Every waste tonne is paid for but sells nothing; rises as pits deepen.

specific_drivers: Head grade; grade decline; dilution; cut-off-grade policy
rationale: Lower grade => more tonnes mined/milled per unit metal, amplifying every other unit cost.

specific_drivers: Crush/grind energy; ore hardness (Bond Wi); metallurgical recovery; refractoriness
rationale: Dominates processing cost for hard-rock metals; recovery loss directly raises cost/unit metal.

specific_drivers: HPAL vs RKEF (Ni); flotation vs leach (Cu); free-mill vs refractory (Au); spodumene vs brine (Li); separation (REE)
rationale: Route choice can swing total-to-metal cost more than the mining step itself.

specific_drivers: In-pit haulage; road; rail; port & shiploading; ocean freight; conveyor/pipeline
rationale: Can exceed mine-gate cost for bulk, low-value commodities (coal, iron ore, bauxite, aggregates, potash).

specific_drivers: Plant & fleet capacity; utilisation; fixed-cost dilution
rationale: Larger, steadier throughput spreads fixed cost and lowers unit cost.

specific_drivers: Explosives; grinding media; tyres; lime, cyanide, collectors, frothers, acid, caustic soda, soda ash
rationale: Explosives = small energy share but large cost share; reagent intensity scales with grade/mineralogy.

specific_drivers: Sustaining maintenance; availability/utilisation; MARC contracts; spares & tyres
rationale: Availability swings hit both unit cost and lost production.

specific_drivers: Supply, desalination, pumping, treatment, dewatering, scarcity
rationale: Rising constraint (esp. arid copper/lithium regions); desalination + pumping is capital- & energy-heavy.

specific_drivers: TSF construction; dam safety & monitoring; waste-rock; red mud; phosphogypsum; radioactive residues
rationale: Post-Brumadinho (GISTM) standards materially raised cost; radioactive residues (U/Th) add regulation.

specific_drivers: Ad-valorem/specific royalties; export duties; cess; DMF/NMET (India); windfall taxes
rationale: Highly jurisdiction-specific; can be double-digit % of revenue or a fixed $/t.

specific_drivers: Permitting; emissions monitoring; carbon price; biodiversity offsets; community programs
rationale: Rising structurally; carbon-price exposure increasingly material for energy-intensive processing.

specific_drivers: Progressive rehab; closure provisioning & financial assurance
rationale: Accrued over life; large legacy liability for big-footprint surface operations (lignite, iron ore).

specific_drivers: Land acquisition; resettlement & rehabilitation; consent delays; CRZ/forest clearance
rationale: Especially significant in India; delays sterilise reserves and add cost.

specific_drivers: Local-cost/USD-revenue FX exposure; input-cost escalation
rationale: A stronger producer currency (AUD, ZAR, BRL, INR) raises USD unit costs with no operational change.

specific_drivers: Depth; ground conditions; hydrogeology; ore continuity/complexity; seismicity; gas (coal)
rationale: Drives method, support, dewatering, dilution — all unit-cost levers.

specific_drivers: Co-product revenue (Au/Ag/Mo/Co/Pb/Re/Se/Te) netted against primary metal
rationale: Can halve or zero net C1; the main reason 2025 copper/precious costs fell.

specific_drivers: Ga, Ge, In, Re, Te, Se, Cd, Hf, Bi recovered only from host-metal streams
rationale: These have NO standalone mining cost; supply is inelastic to their own price — a structural vulnerability.

specific_drivers: Sustaining capex (feeds AISC); deferred stripping; fleet replacement
rationale: The bridge from C1 cash cost to AISC.

specific_drivers: Power grid, roads, water, port (greenfield vs brownfield); permitting timelines; political stability
rationale: Greenfield projects in remote/developing regions carry far higher build & operating cost.

specific_drivers: Single-country dominance (China REE/Ga/Ge/W; DRC Co; Indonesia Ni); export controls & bans
rationale: Sets price floors/spikes independent of mine cost; central to 'critical mineral' designation.
